mechanism_narrative: Increases in the federal funds rate raise the discount rate for equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >65% of periods following a 25bps rate increase.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals weakening labor demand, which reduces consumer spending forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >70% of instances where the unemployment rate increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and moderation in consumer price inflation indicates robust demand growth without triggering inflationary fears, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where retail sales growth exceeds 2% annually while CPI inflation remains below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an early-cycle expansion reflects improving capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns show no statistically significant positive bias (p > 0.10) during periods classified as early-cycle expansion with rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, which mechanically supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where housing starts increase by more than 5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 2.5% annualized threshold forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of periods where year-over-year CPI growth surpasses 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms sustainable labor market expansion, boosting corporate revenue expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where payroll growth exceeds 150k monthly while the unemployment rate falls.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth during a late-cycle peak signals diminishing marginal returns on capital and impending earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns show positive mean direction in >65% of quarters where real GDP growth decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points.
